summary:
Teaching medical students to investigate medication errors improves their attitudes toward patient safety by redefining errors as learnable experiences rather than failures. This shift promotes psychological safety and develops resilient, accountable professionals through experience-based learning.  

The summary is concise, focused solely on core clinical implications related to attitude changes due to education about medication errors.
